← the Atlas

Faithful History Render — Stop Reflowing Recorded Terminal Output

Features·seedling·proposed·JUN 26, 2026

The copy-mode pager (and the attach snapshot) corrupt TUI history by replaying recorded bytes at a foreign width. Render history as fixed physical rows at its historical width instead; never reflow.

Contents

  • User-facing description
  • Architecture-level changes
    • Why reflow is the wrong model (the root cause, reproduced)
    • The model: history is fixed physical rows, never reflowed
    • Blast-radius map (every render-at-foreign-width site)
  • Implementation details
    • 1. Fixed-row ANSI serializer (packages/kaval/src/transcript/render.ts)
    • 2. history() renders faithful, width-locked (transcript.ts)
    • 3. Pager displays at content width with horizontal scroll (HistoryPager.tsx)
    • 4. Attach resize-first (Terminal.tsx, mobile shares this path)
    • 5. Docs + tests
    • Design-philosophy check

User-facing description

Opening ⏱ History on a terminal that ran an agent (Claude Code, or any TUI) shows garbled, overlapping text — words doubled, spaces replaced by stray characters, fragments stacked on the left edge. The deeper the history, the worse it reads. The same flaw flashes on reconnect and on mobile, where the inherited scrollback briefly scrambles before the live screen repaints.

Germinating... thinking with xhigh effort and a very long tail to push…
                            ↑ reader width = 90  (emit width was 120)
\r  →  returns to col 0 of the WRAPPED TAIL, not the logical line start
Germinating... almost done          ← overwrites the WRONG physical row
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
result the user sees (your screenshot):
  Re Refocusing on the the2concrete5deliverable:αthetdraft PR …
  or  reshrm  gentacode s  de-Pierre'd).  ← overlapping fragments

The fix makes the pager show history exactly as the terminal drew it — every box, spinner frame, and indented block intact — by rendering at the width the content was actually emitted at and never re-wrapping it. When that width is wider than the modal, you scroll horizontally (like less -S or tmux copy-mode), the same way you’d scroll a wide file. Nothing is squeezed or re-flowed.

render at the HISTORICAL width (120), emit FIXED ROWS (hard \r\n):
  Germinating... almost doneth xhigh effort and a very long tail…   ✓ exact
display: pager xterm sized to the content (120); scroll → horizontally
  ┌──────────────── modal viewport (≈90 cols) ───────────┊── scroll ──┐
  │ Germinating... almost doneth xhigh effort and a very │ long tail… │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┊───────────┘

No new buttons; the pager just stops corrupting TUI history. PDF export is already faithful and is unchanged. kaval-tui attach is already correct.

Architecture-level changes

Why reflow is the wrong model (the root cause, reproduced)

A terminal’s recorded output is a stream of bytes that includes \r and cursor-positioning — every TUI redraws in place. Those bytes are only meaningful at the width they were emitted at: a \r returns to column 0 of the current physical row, and which physical row that is depends on where lines wrapped. Replay the same bytes into a terminal of a different width and the wrap points move, so every in-place redraw lands on the wrong row — overlap, duplication, garbage. Reproduced against @xterm/headless@6.0.0:

Replay width Germinating… line (104 chars) then \r + almost done
120 (emit width) Germinating... almost done overwrites the line start ✓
90 (foreign) the line wraps; \r hits the wrapped tail → almost done overwrites the wrong row ✗

Plain text reflows fine; cursor-addressed content cannot be reflowed — and kolu terminals are overwhelmingly agent TUIs, so this is the common case. The only faithful rendering is at the emit width. PDF export already does exactly this (renderFaithful at the historical per-epoch width); the pager does not.

The model: history is fixed physical rows, never reflowed

A terminal’s scrollback is a grid of fixed-width rows. The lossy step is re-wrapping them. So:

  1. Render each recorded physical row at its historical width (the per-resize-epoch width the faithful renderer already establishes), reconstructed by replaying the DATA at that width so all the \r/cursor moves land correctly — exactly the final screen the terminal showed.
  2. Emit it width-locked — a new fixed-row ANSI serializer writes each physical row with its SGR followed by a hard \r\n. Because the wrap is now a real newline (not a soft wrap the consumer re-derives), the bytes render identically at any display width. A 120-col epoch and an 80-col epoch can sit in one display buffer with no cross-contamination.
  3. Solve width at the display layer — the pager xterm is sized to the content’s width (the widest epoch loaded); the modal scrolls horizontally past its viewport. Width is a free choice only at display time, never during recording or render.

This converges the pager onto the same faithful path as export — one rendering model, two consumers (a bounded page vs. the whole stream).

Blast-radius map (every render-at-foreign-width site)

Site Client Reflows at foreign width? Action
HistoryPager → history() → renderReflow(readerWidth) web pager Yes — persistent Fix: faithful fixed-row render at historical width
Attach snapshot serialize({scrollback}) @ mirror width → client xterm @ fitted width web · mobile Yes — scrollback persists, screen self-corrects Fix: resize-first (match kaval-tui); deep history lives in the now-faithful pager
exportHistory → renderFaithful PDF No (historical width) none — already correct
kaval-tui attach (resize-before-attach) TUI No (matched) none — the pattern to copy
Live region (post-attach output) all No (PTY resized to client width) none — new output is always matched

The mirror has one width (shared, last-resize-wins); that is unchanged. The multi-client width fight is pre-existing and out of scope.

Why the live terminal's scrollback is not the target

The live Terminal.tsx is one xterm that must wrap new output at the client’s width, so its scrollback is xterm’s own reflowing buffer — we don’t fork that. PR2 already moved deep history off it into the pager (the live mirror keeps only HOT_WINDOW ≈ 500 lines). So the fix is: make the pager faithful (deep, persistent, read-on-purpose) and make attach resize-first (so the visible screen is correct on join); the shallow live scrollback re-wrap is bounded and the accurate record is one keypress away in the pager.

Implementation details

Build-ready for an implementing agent. One PR; the pieces are coupled (the serializer + the verb + the client display move together) and nothing ships half-faithful.

1. Fixed-row ANSI serializer (packages/kaval/src/transcript/render.ts)

Add serializeFixedRows(term, fromRow) — walk buffer.active from fromRow to the last non-blank row and, per physical row, emit the row’s cells as SGR-runs (coalesce adjacent equal-attribute cells into one \x1b[…m run; reset at row end) followed by a literal \r\n. Reuse the cell→SGR mapping @xterm/addon-serialize already implements rather than hand-rolling color math — extract or vendor its per-cell attribute diff (fg/bg/bold/italic/underline/inverse/dim/strike), so the fixed-row output matches export’s colors exactly. Contract test: a row stream serialized fixed-row, written into a width-W and a width-2W xterm, yields byte-identical translateToString per row (width-locked — the property the whole fix rests on).

2. history() renders faithful, width-locked (transcript.ts)

  • Drop the width parameter’s role as a reflow width. history() pages the same checkpoint-to-checkpoint byte range, but renders the page through the faithful segmentation (faithfulSegments per resize-epoch) and emits each segment via serializeFixedRows, concatenated. The seed rows are dropped exactly as today (seedBoundaryRow, including the deferred-wrap +1 from the seam fix).
  • Add contentWidth to the ok arm of HistoryResult (+ the wire schema): the max epoch width in the page, so the client sizes its xterm. nextCursor / floorEvicted / evicted semantics are unchanged.
  • renderReflow loses its only caller and is deleted (the reflow model is the defect — make it unspellable, not merely unused). searchHistory already joins logical lines width-independently and does not display, so it keeps a plain replay; only the rendered paths change.

3. Pager displays at content width with horizontal scroll (HistoryPager.tsx)

  • Remove the FitAddon width coupling: the pager xterm’s cols is set to the page’s contentWidth (grown as older, wider epochs load), not the modal width. Keep rows fitted to the modal height.
  • Wrap the xterm host in an overflow-x: auto container so the modal scrolls horizontally; keep the existing vertical backfill-on-scroll. The byte cursors are reflow-stable, so re-paging is unaffected.
  • Drop the resize→refetch path (no width refetch needed — content width is historical, not reader-derived); a height change still just re-fits rows.
  • Risk — search highlight offsets: the in-view SearchAddon re-finds matches in the rendered buffer; fixed rows preserve the buffer text, so this is unaffected — pin it with a test that a hit highlights on the correct row after the switch.

4. Attach resize-first (Terminal.tsx, mobile shares this path)

Reorder attach so the client resizes the PTY to its fitted width before reading the snapshot (the kaval-tui pattern at attach.ts:243): fit() → resize({id, cols, rows}) → attach → write snapshot. The snapshot is then serialized at the client’s width, so the visible screen is faithful on join. No change to the mirror or the live stream. Risk — multi-client width thrash: resize-first makes a joining client claim the shared width; this matches today’s last-write-wins behavior (a join already resized on first fit), so it is not a new regression — note it, don’t expand scope.

5. Docs + tests

  • Update kaval-memory-architecture.mdx (the parent): the pager is faithful at historical width, not reflow-to-current; the “reflow-to-current (pager) vs faithful (export)” split collapses to “faithful everywhere; display chooses width.”
  • Tests (kaval): the fixed-row width-lock contract test (§1); a history() page of a recorded TUI stream (\r-redraw + cursor moves) asserted byte-identical to the live terminal’s final screen at the historical width (the regression guard for this bug — red on renderReflow, green on faithful); a multi-epoch page (resize mid-history) rendering each epoch faithfully in one buffer.
  • Evidence: the pager showing a real Claude Code session’s history intact, plus the horizontal-scroll interaction, captured on a pu box.

Design-philosophy check

  • Fail-fast / no fallback: renderReflow is deleted, not left as a toggle — the corrupting path becomes unspellable. No “reflow vs faithful” knob.
  • Reuse the source of truth: the pager adopts export’s faithful renderer and @xterm/addon-serialize’s cell→SGR mapping; attach adopts kaval-tui’s resize-first. No parallel mechanism.
  • Electricity / boundaries: the fixed-row serializer is a leaf in the transcript render module beside its faithful sibling — a bounded algorithm, not a new package.
← the Atlasgithub ↗
